Looking at the original buggy code more carefully:
```java
for (int pt = 0; pt < consumed; pt++) {
    if (pos < len - 2) {
        pos += Character.charCount(Character.codePointAt(input, pos));
    } else {
        pos++;

}
pos--;
```

This code was trying to advance the position for each code point consumed, but it was doing it incorrectly. The variable `pos` in the while loop represents a code point index (not a character index). So when we call `Character.codePointAt(input, pos)`, this is actually wrong since `pos` is a code point index, not a character index.

The method `Character.codePointAt(CharSequence seq, int index)` expects the `index` to be a character index, not a code point index. This is the root of the issue!

```java
/**
 * Translate a set of codepoints, represented by an int index into a CharSequence,

public abstract int translate(CharSequence input, int index, Writer out) throws IOException;
```
